title: Accessible static site (WCAG 2.1 AA) + persistent "not medical advice"/provenance footers
context: The educational content authored in M1 and M2 must be delivered in an accessible, printable, offline-capable format. This task builds the complete static site from the M1/M2 content corpus, meeting WCAG 2.1 AA and carrying the mandatory 'not medical advice' and per-page provenance footers on every patient-facing page. The site has no accounts, no backend, and no identifying analytics.
objective: Build and merge a complete, accessible static site rendering the M1/M2 content corpus, meeting WCAG 2.1 AA, with persistent 'not medical advice' and per-page review/provenance footers on all patient-facing content.
acceptanceCriteria: All M1 and M2 modules, worksheets, glossary, and walkthroughs are rendered in the static site with correct navigation.
WCAG 2.1 AA compliance verified by automated tool (e.g. axe-core) with zero critical violations; manual keyboard-navigation and screen-reader spot-check documented.
Persistent 'education only — not medical advice' banner/notice present on every patient-facing page, not dismissable.
Per-page provenance footer links to the sources.yaml entries for that page's citations and shows last-reviewed date and reviewer roles.
Site is offline-capable (e.g. service worker or printable-first design) and renders well in print/PDF view.
No personal data collected, no identifying cookies or analytics; any usage counting is cookieless and aggregate-only.
Lighthouse accessibility score >= 90 on all key pages.
CI build produces the site artefact and the deployment step is documented.
resources: PLAN.md (§ Solution approach — Static site; § Success metrics — Metric 8 accessibility)
output: A merged PR delivering the complete static site build with WCAG 2.1 AA compliance, persistent 'not medical advice' and provenance footers, and offline/printable capability.
